Module:
Advanced Databases  |
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEF11
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
1h30  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
This course focuses on essential concepts and technologies for the design, management and operation of modern database systems, covering both theoretical and practical aspects, enabling students to update and deepen their knowledge of databases.
Recommended Prerequisites
Basic concepts of databases, SQL language, relational algebra
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to Databases
Fundamentals review: relational model, DBMS, query languages
Types of databases: relational, NoSQL, object-oriented, etc.
DBMS architecture: client-server, distributed architectures
Transaction concepts, concurrency, and data recovery
Advanced SQL Programming
SQL review: joins, subqueries, aggregate functions, views, stored procedures and functions
Advanced SQL: Triggers, error handling and management
NoSQL data manipulation languages: JSONiq, Cypher
The Object-Relational Model
Presentation of the Object model
Presentation of the Relational-Object model
Object-Relational model concepts (complex types, inheritance...)
Querying Object-Relational databases (SQL3)
NoSQL Databases
Introduction to NoSQL databases: types (key-value, document, graph, column)
NoSQL data models: advantages and disadvantages
Case studies: MongoDB, Cassandra, Neo4j
Distributed Databases
Architecture of distributed systems
Replication and partitioning techniques
Data consistency in distributed environments: ACID vs BASE
Cloud Databases
Service models: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Cloud computing and databases
Security considerations in the cloud
Database Performance and Security
SQL query optimization
Indexing and access strategies
Security: access control, data encryption, audit
Security management in distributed databases
Assessment
Semester exam (in-person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — continuous tests, personal work
Bibliographic References
R. Elmasri & S. Navathe (2016). Fundamentals of Database Systems, 7th Edition, Pearson.
T. Connolly & C. Begg (2014). Database Systems: A Practical Approach to Design, Implementation, and Management, 6th Edition, Pearson.
Christian Soutou (2013). SQL pour Oracle. Editions Eyrolles.
Silberschatz, H., Korth, S., & Sudarshan, S. (2019). Database System Concepts, 7th Edition, McGraw-Hill.
Mohamed Fadhel Saad (2016). PL/SQL sous Oracle 12c – Guide du développeur.
R. G. G. Cattell (1994). Object Data Management. Addison-Wesley.
P. Selmer (2012). NoSQL Stores and Data Analytics Tools. In Advances in Data Management.
